label_source_note: Labels for the 20 ultra-gal-origin panels are inherited from experiment/02-vision-audit-pilot labeled-set-v1.json and refined with v1 taxonomy sub-categories drawn from projects/ultra-gal-origin/audits/pages-01-07-audit-2026-05-16.md (Matt-validated). Labels for the 10 chun-li-test panels are mapped from projects/chun-li-test/continuity-report.md (Matt-validated, 2026-05-22). Labels for the 6 ref-sheet assets are mapped from projects/ultra-gal-origin/audits/ref-sheets-qa-2026-05-17.md (Matt-validated). Where both Magnamus-discord-pattern and Matt-audit-mapped labels exist for the same panel, both are captured with `labeler` distinguishing the source — no averaging.

label_source_provenance.matt-mapped-from-audit: Defect extracted from a per-project audit doc authored by a Comic-production QA subagent and committed by Matt without modification. Same provenance Exp 02 used.
label_source_provenance.matt-direct: Defect Matt called out in his own voice (e.g., the 'USER-FLAGGED' tag on p03-01) or codified into a project rule (L21-L24, L33).
label_source_provenance.magnamus-discord-pattern: Defect class Magnamus has surfaced repeatedly in Discord notes per docs/experiments/02-vision-audit-pilot/notebooklm-brief.md. Pattern-level, not panel-specific — used to validate that the taxonomy covers a class even when no labeled-set panel exhibits it.
label_source_provenance.exp-02-inherited: Label originally appears in docs/experiments/02-vision-audit-pilot/labeled-set-v1.json. The original Exp 02 labels were derived from the same matt-mapped-from-audit source; this provenance tag is kept so re-scoring of Exp 02 predictions against this labeled set is honest about the chain of custody.

split.holdout_selection_method: Stratified to include at least one BAD and one GOOD; spans hair (p06-04, p07-01), character_count (p05-02), and a GOOD (p06-03). Held out BEFORE any rubric tuning happens against this labeled set. Exp 02's iterations v1-v5 WERE tuned on the full 20-panel set, so when we re-score Exp 02 predictions on this holdout, those predictions are NOT model-free — we are measuring how a previously-tuned model output scores against the refined taxonomy. We document this explicitly in metrics-v1.md.

future_expansion_set.note: These 10 chun-li-test panels and 6 ref-sheet assets are recorded as labels-only entries because the panel PNGs are not in this checkout (chun-li-test panels live in the user's Flow cloud project; ref-sheets are auditable files but distinct from story panels). They are NOT in the training/holdout split for this experiment. They become measurable once the PNGs are pulled down OR when ref-sheet support lands in the audit pipeline.

coverage_gaps_vs_taxonomy.note: All four composite.* leaves remain unmeasured — same gap Exp 02 flagged as the biggest blocker to shipping. The taxonomy now NAMES these classes correctly even when the labeled set has zero examples, which is itself progress: the rubric can ask the model about them and we can begin to accumulate positives in production.

labeler_disagreement_summary.note_on_disagreement: In this checkout there is no Magnamus-direct panel-level label list — Magnamus's input is captured as defect-CLASS patterns (Source 4 in raw-defects.md), not panel-by-panel verdicts. The 5 Magnamus pattern entries therefore inform the taxonomy but cannot be compared per-panel against Matt's labels. The labeled-set structure leaves room for Magnamus-direct labels to be added as a new labeler row per panel (the `labels_by_labeler` array is multi-entry by design) — a future hand-labeling pass by Magnamus is the right unlock here. The recommendation file flags this.

methodology_notes: All Exp 02 labels are preserved verbatim under the `exp_02_categories` field on each ultra-gal panel. The forward mapping from Exp 02 categories to v1 sub-categories is in taxonomy-v1.md.

Confidence values reflect the audit doc's language: a flatly-stated defect (e.g., 'duplicate "OH." bubble') gets 1.0; a hedged note (e.g., 'possibly missing or cropped') gets 0.6-0.7; a SOFT continuity finding gets 0.4-0.7 per the report's own classification.

Same-panel multi-defect labels are listed explicitly rather than collapsed — p10-01 carries both `character.costume_design_drift` AND `character.costume_state_drift_in_scene` because the audit doc notes both.
Future labelers can add their entries by appending to `labels_by_labeler` without modifying existing entries — this is how Magnamus-direct labels would be incorporated in a follow-up.
